note: Letta sleep-time vein rich; surfaces async-sleeptime-consolidation initiative + the loop-governor for CG-agentic-tool-loop.

claim: NET-NEW (H/M) headline: a background agent fires AFTER the foreground step, reorganizing transcripts via tool-chain (store_memories->rethink->finish), spawned per-main-agent, async. Internal has NO background reorganization agent; reconsolidation-bridge.ts is synchronous on-save.
maps_to: new async consolidation pass (cadence-driven) — no internal equivalent

claim: NET-NEW (M/S, cheap): persistent turns_counter gates background work (turns_counter % frequency == 0, default 5) to amortize reorganization cost. Internal pressure-monitor gates on token-ratio, retention sweep is cron/trigger — neither is turn-count cadence.

NET-NEW (M/M) — LOOP-GOVERNOR for CG-agentic-tool-loop: strict tool-call DAG (Init->Child->Continue->Terminal) forces a full cycle, no early bail. This is exactly the bounding mechanism iter-22 found CG-agentic-tool-loop lacks (greenfield governor) — Letta's DAG is a ready template.
maps_to: handlers/memory-context.ts (governor for an agentic strategy) — no tool-call DAG today

claim: NET-NEW (M/M): store_memory(start_index,end_index,context) lets the LLM slice raw transcript by index-range and store the chunk as an archival passage. Internal chunking-orchestrator chunks by markdown structure, not LLM-selected ranges.

NET-NEW (L/M, lower priority): sequence-based undo/redo stack (BlockHistory, sequence_number) with linear-history truncation. Internal checkpoints.ts is snapshot-based (full DB copy), not a navigable sequence stack. Lower leverage — snapshots already work.

EXTENDS (M/S): passage tags in BOTH a JSON column AND a PassageTag junction table -> DISTINCT/EXISTS filtering + tag-count aggregation. Internal memory_index.trigger_phrases is a text column; no junction for structured tag aggregation.
